calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Receipts On Distribution
Off Calendar Apr-28-2010 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-25-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Lamerdin for the Attorney General's Office. Raymond Levy in Propria Persona.

Mr. Levy addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes and explained the extent of services he provided to support the fee request. Mr. Lamerdin objected to the fees. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-03-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Lamerdin for the State of California Department of Justice.

Mr. Levy stated that he intended to close out the trust at the end of the next accounting year end. Mr. Lamerdin requested the matter be set for six months out. The Court granted the petition, (subject to review of the order). The matter will be set out on the next scheduled accounting date.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Sara Lerschen, CSR#6213. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. McDaniels.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-20-2009 Continued To Aug-03-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-11-2009 Continued To Jul-20-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Raymond Levy, Esq. present in pro per as Trustee. Kelvin Gong, Esq. jpresent on behalf of The Office of the Attorney General.

Mr. Levy stated that as Trustee has sole discretion as to the manner of the distribution of the assets from the trust, and is currently working with the Masonic Foundation, distribution will be made in the next couple of months. Mr. Gong stated the concern of why there was no distribution at all, since Mr. Levy has the power and responsibility, he needs to exercise his discretion of distribution to the charitable beneficiaries, further, he is objecting to the trustee fees requested. The Court acknowledged the Trustee discretion, but voiced the concern over the period of time and the percentage of distributions made, the fees paid to him self, and to bring these to conclusion.
By July 14, 2009 Mr. Levy to file a supplement details over a comprehensive distribution plans with dates, amount, to whom or which beneficiaries.
Matter is continued to July 20, 2009 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.
Mr. Gong requested Mr. Levy to send notices to his local office and not to Sacramento.

Comm. Sue Kaplan, CSR: LaVena Ward #7077, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, Bailiff: Deputy Gomez.
